steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states Nadia Joy Rivera-Santos's goal: avoid harsh synthetic dyes that damage the environment. The second sentence describes her resulting action: experimenting with natural dye methods. The transition must show the action flows from the goal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — With this in mind,: Signals that the action in sentence 2 follows directly from the concern stated in sentence 1. Goal → motivated action. This is exactly the relationship here. ✓
  • Choice B — To sum up,: Signals a summary of preceding points, but there is only one prior sentence and nothing to sum up. ✗
  • Choice C — Nevertheless,: Signals a contrast or concession. Experimenting with natural dyes is not a contradiction of wanting to avoid harmful dyes — it is a direct response to it. ✗
  • Choice D — In actuality,: Signals a correction of a mistaken assumption. No mistaken assumption appears in the passage. ✗
A is correct.
